lessonTitle: Defining Transitions and Identifying Barriers to Successful Transitions in Sickle Cell Disease

items.text: Define transitions and highlight trends specific to Adolescents and Young Adults (AYAs) with Sickle Cell Disease
Identify physical, psychological, and social challenges faced by youth with Sickle Cell Disease in relation to transition from paediatric to adult care
List barriers and facilitators to transitions of Adolescents and Young Adults (AYAs) with Sickle Cell Disease

data.html:
Introduction

Living with Sickle Cell Disease (SCD) comes with multiple challenges in addition to physical pain. For instance, AYAs with SCD experience many life changes during the transition period.

In addition to moving from one healthcare setting to another, many AYAs are also balancing enrolling in post secondary school, adjusting to young adulthood, or starting a new job.

The goal of this lesson is to identify a list of possible approaches to support AYAs in developing transition skills that will support youth as they go through the multifaceted changes during the transition period.

slides.title: Defining Transitions in Sickle Cell Disease

blocks.title: Defining Transitions in Sickle Cell Disease

data.html:
Defining Transitions in Sickle Cell Disease

Transition in healthcare involves addressing medical, psychosocial, and educational needs as care shifts from child and family-centered to adult-oriented.

This process should begin around age 12, to start emphasizing self-management skills, vocational planning, and adolescent health education.

The exact count of sickle cell disease (SCD) patients in Ontario, Canada, remains unknown. A 2023 study estimated that about 1 in 4200 people in Ontario had SCD from 2007/08 to 2016/17. The median age among this population was 24 years old. (1)

slides.title: Current State of Healthcare Transition for AYAs with SCD

blocks.title: Current State of Healthcare Transition for AYAs with SCD

data.html:
Current State of Healthcare Transition for AYAs with SCD

AYAs with SCD between 18-30 years old, compared to other age groups, have been shown to be more vulnerable during healthcare transition due to:

items.text: unpredictable symptoms
frequent pain episodes
high healthcare utilization
more rehospitalizations related to pain management
higher morbidity/mortality rates

data.html: A study of data between 1999-2009 revealed higher mortality rates among AYAs aged 20-24 years, aligning with the transition from paediatric to adult care, with rates of 0.6/100,000 for 15-19-year-olds and 1.4/100,000 for 20-24-year-olds.

images.caption: Click on the image to zoom in. Photo credit: Brown Med-Peds, n.d. This figure presents the age specific-mortality rates subdivided into four time periods (1979–1988, 1989–1998, 1999–2003, and 2004–2009). Note the decline in mortality over the years for ages < 14 years old and then starts to fluctuate and increase as age increases.

data.question: Mortality rates increase dramatically after childhood between the ages of 20-24.
data.feedback_correct: True. Mortality rates increase dramatically after childhood between the ages of 20-24.

data.question: The transition period may risk treatment delays and service disengagement for young adults.
data.feedback_correct: True. The transition period may risk treatment delays and service disengagement for young adults.

data.question: AYAs with SCD are at lower risk of vulnerability during transition due to the unpredictable nature of their symptoms, frequent pain episodes, high healthcare utilization, more rehospitalizations related to pain management, and higher morbidity/mortality rates.
data.feedback_correct: False. AYAs with SCD are at higher risk of vulnerability during transition due to the unpredictable nature of their symptoms, frequent pain episodes, high healthcare utilization, more rehospitalizations related to pain management, and higher morbidity/mortality rates.

data.html:
Navigating Multiple Transitions

AYAs with SCD face multiple simultaneous life transitions alongside transitioning from paediatric to adult healthcare services.

School SCD complications like pain, cognitive deficits, and frequent hospitalizations harm school attendance and performance. Transitioning from high school to college can be tough for AYAs with SCD without proper planning. A study found 60% of 12-20-year-olds with SCD said it impacted their education, with 40% repeating grades, and those experiencing more hospitalizations reported it leading to lower grades.

Social and Psychological AYA also express that they have insufficient time to develop relationships with peers in their early years and limited opportunities to engage in peer activities during the transition period, leading to feelings of isolation, fatigue, and mental distress.

There needs to be an establishment of trust between HCP and SCD patient to remove barriers in accessing care.

There needs to be an establishment of trust between HCP and SCD patient to remove barriers in accessing care.

Tips for Effective Communication

Photo credit: Patient Voices Network, 2021

Effective partnerships start with getting to know each other and working together to leverage experiences and abilities.

These tips help create a comfortable environment with shared understanding and communication which is open, honest and respectful.

slides.title: Strategies to Reduce Barriers to Transition

blocks.title: Strategies to Reduce Barriers to Transition

data.html:
Strategies to Reduce Barriers to Transition

Healthcare transition programs aid in preparing, transferring, and integrating young adults into adult care settings.

The 6 Core Elements of Health Care Transition is a set of principles and guidelines developed by the American Academy of Pediatrics, the American Academy of Family Physicians, and the American College of Physicians.

These elements aim to ensure a smooth transition from paediatric to adult healthcare for individuals with special healthcare needs.

Further information on implementation and guides can be found here: https://www.gottransition.org/

The 6 Core Elements of Health Care Transitions

True or False?

items.text: There are a number of gaps in the transition from paediatric centred to adult centred care.
Communication style and creating feelings of psychological safety with patients is key to creating trusting relationships and engagement in care.
The 6 core elements of healthcare transitions are: transition policy, transition planning, transfer of care, transfer readiness, transfer completion, and transition tracking and monitoring

candidates.suggestion: The 'Ethical and Cultural Considerations' section contains 8 images with no accompanying prose explaining what each image depicts. Consider whether the images carry explanatory text within the images themselves (carousel/infographic slides), or whether additional prose is available in source material. If the images are self-contained infographics, a gallery is appropriate.
candidates.reason: 8 consecutive images with no prose — source has only the bare section header 'Ethical and Cultural Considerations'. The trust/tips text that follows relates to the theme but was not explicitly a caption for these images.

candidates.suggestion: The 'The 6 Core Elements of Health Care Transitions' bold header and the standalone 'True or False?' bold line appear as bare headings with no content or options below them. These appear to be EdApp interactive/accordion placeholders that lost their content on export. If future source material reveals the 6 elements, they should be added as a content_list.
candidates.reason: Source MD has '**The 6 Core Elements of Health Care Transitions**' followed immediately by '**True or False?**' with no list rows, options, or explanation — treated as rich_text headers per spec instruction, not fabricated content.
